senses.definition: the act or sound of prising or prissing; a sibilant or hissing sound similar to a hiss
examples: The cat made a soft prissing as it moved through the leaves.
Her skirt rustled with a prissing sound as she walked.

senses.definition: too concerned with small details of appearance or behavior; easily offended or overproper
examples: She is a bit prissy about her furniture and will not let anyone spill coffee nearby.
Don't be so prissy—just relax and have some fun.

senses.definition: an oily, colorless liquid hydrocarbon of the terpane class found in crude oil and known for high chemical resistance
examples: Pristane is used in some industrial lubricant formulations.
The sample contained notable amounts of pristane.

senses.definition: plural of pristane; multiple pristane molecules in a sample
examples: The report measured several pristanes in the sample.
Researchers compared the pristanes with other hydrocarbons.

senses.definition: a fish genus in the family Lutjanidae or related taxonomic contexts; may refer to tropical sea fish species
examples: The fisherman offered a variety of pristav species.
Taxonomic keys distinguish pristav from related genera.

senses.definition: a taxonomic or regional term; may appear as a misspelling or variant of related genera; not widely used in modern English
examples: The guide mentioned pristaw as a historical name in local taxonomy.

senses.definition: in perfect condition; unspoiled and clean
examples: The cabin was in pristine condition after a fresh cleaning.
They kept the hiking trail almost pristine.

senses.definition: in a pristine way; perfectly clean or unspoiled
examples: The garden was pristinely kept, with every leaf in place.
The archive was pristinely organized.

senses.definition: the quality of being pristine; pure, untouched, or in original condition
examples: The pristineness of the environment attracted researchers.
They preserved the pristineness of the artifact.

senses.definition: a family of fish known as pink snapper or related species within the order Perciformes
examples: The prstipomatidae family includes several reef-associated species.

senses.definition: a former or alternate spelling for a fish family (synonym or related taxon to Pristipomatidae)
examples: Taxonomic revisions sometimes merge pristipomidae with pristipomatidae.

senses.definition: a genus of sawfish found in tropical and subtropical seas
examples: The Pristis genus includes several large sawfish species.
Fisheries monitor Pristis populations to protect them.

senses.definition: an extinct genus of prehistoric jawed vertebrates known from fossil records
examples: Fossils of Pristodus date from the Paleozoic era.

senses.definition: to poke or jab; to snap or trim with a sharp, quick motion
examples: He pritched the knot apart with a small knife.
The tailor pritch-cut the fabric to shape.

senses.definition: a plant genus in the Arecaceae (palm) family; includes certain palm trees
examples: Pritchardia species are native to Pacific islands.

senses.definition: a tool with a small cylindrical punch used in leatherworking or metalworking to punch holes or studs
examples: The saddler used a pritchel to punch a hole in the belt.
A sturdy pritchel helps align rivets precisely.

senses.definition: an archaic or poetic form of please; used to request something
examples: Prithee, tell me the truth.

senses.definition: idling chatter; trivial talk; prattle
examples: The crowd spoke in prittle about the latest fashions.
Enough of this prittle; get to the point.

senses.definition: a model line of Toyota hybrid cars; also used as a generic term for a small car in some contexts
examples: He bought a Prius because of its fuel efficiency.
The Prius is popular among eco-conscious drivers.

senses.definition: short for private, or a private person; also used in abbreviations or nicknames
examples: He kept his priv thoughts to himself.
The pub's priv room is reserved for members.

senses.definition: plural of privacy; different aspects of personal privacy
examples: They discussed the privacies of the individuals involved.
Legal protections include rights to several privacies.

senses.definition: the state or quality of being private or private life; privacy
examples: The term privacity appears in some legal texts as a synonym for privacy.

senses.definition: the right to keep personal information and life private; solitude or freedom from public attention
examples: Everyone deserves some privacy at home.
She values her privacy and does not share much online.

senses.definition: a person who is private or a private individual; in some contexts a private secretary or partner in a private enterprise; also a proper noun in some languages
examples: The privado in the document refers to a private individual.

senses.definition: plural of privado; private individuals or private entities
examples: A group of privadoes attended the conference.
